title: Investigate, identify, and fix admin deposit page missing records and wrong status display
issue_description: Investigate an issue where the admin deposit page may not be showing all deposits and may not correctly display whether deposits are pending or completed. Systems involved may include AlgonneyPay, treasury events, algonney.com backend, database queries, admin API endpoints, and frontend table/status mapping. Work step by step with one and only one step per cycle. First investigate the full deposit data path end-to-end, then identify and prove the exact root cause, then propose and implement the fix, and finally validate that the page shows all deposits correctly and displays the correct pending/completed status. Changes are allowed only after the issue is clearly proven. Prefer read-only investigation first, then move to fix mode only when the root cause is confirmed. Stop only when the issue is fully resolved, blocked, or requires explicit human approval for a risky action.
issue_seed_prompt: Investigate, identify, and fix admin deposit page missing records and wrong status display

history.step_title: Trace admin deposit page frontend data source and status mapping
history.prompt_for_claude:
Investigate ONLY the frontend/admin deposit page data flow in read-only mode.

Goal: find the exact admin deposit page component, the API hook/client it uses, the endpoint it calls, the query params it sends, the response fields it renders, and the code that maps backend deposit status into the displayed UI status (pending/completed/etc).

Do not change anything. Do not inspect unrelated systems yet.

Steps:

Locate the admin deposit page component and related table/status-rendering files.

Trace its data source to the hook/service/API client function.

Capture the exact endpoint path and params used.

Identify every response field used for each deposit row.

Identify the exact status mapping logic from raw backend data to displayed label/badge.

Output only:

files inspected

exact frontend call chain (page -> hook -> api client -> endpoint)

exact status mapping code/logic found

any immediate mismatch suspicion, but no fix yet

history.planner_interpretation: Before checking backend, treasury, or database behavior, we need to prove exactly which API endpoint the admin deposit page calls, what request params it sends, which response fields it expects, and how it maps raw deposit status into the displayed pending/completed labels. This establishes the first concrete link in the deposit data path and prevents guessing.
